title: Review-reports directory creation timing unclear in RULES.md Step 3b
description: RULES.md Step 3b doesn't specify when to create .beads/agent-summaries/{epic-id}/review-reports/ relative to spawning Pantry (review mode). reviews.md says 'before composing review prompts' but RULES.md doesn't mention it. Add explicit directory creation to RULES.md Step 3b.

title: Checkpoints epic ID resolution table is overspecified and ambiguous
description: checkpoints.md provides an epic ID resolution table but mixes epic ID format (3-char) with task ID patterns (full format). Does not clearly handle standalone tasks (e.g. hs_website-596y with no .N suffix). Simplify to plain English with annotated examples.

title: Scout doesn't explain _standalone epic lifecycle
description: RULES.md says _standalone persists across sessions (not cleaned up with _session-* artifacts) but Scout template has no mention of this. A fresh Scout might group _standalone tasks from different sessions incorrectly. Add clarification to scout.md about _standalone being persistent.

title: Queen information diet wording ambiguous about agent data files
description: RULES.md Information Diet says 'DO NOT READ: source code, tests, data files, configs...' but dirt-pusher data files are also 'data files'. A fresh Queen might think she cannot read Pantry verdict tables or preview paths. Clarify: the prohibition is on project data files, not orchestration artifacts like data files and verdict tables.

title: RULES.md pantry.md section references not explicit
description: pantry-impl.md references 'pantry.md, Section 1' and pantry-review.md references 'pantry.md, Section 2', but RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b just say '→ templates/pantry.md' without section numbers. Add section references to RULES.md for traceability.

title: Pantry told to read implementation.md but no explanation of what to extract
description: pantry.md Step 1 says 'Read this file: implementation.md' but gives no guidance on what data to extract or how it shapes the Pantry's output. A fresh Pantry agent would read it and not know what to do with it. Clarify: is it informational (absorb the policy) or extractive (pull specific data)?

title: Multi-epic checkpoint artifact naming undefined
description: checkpoints.md says 'Use multi for consolidations spanning multiple epics' but RULES.md doesn't mention multi-epic scenarios. A fresh Pest Control wouldn't know when to use 'multi' vs individual epic IDs. Clarify whether multi-epic reviews are supported and document the naming convention.

title: Dirt-pusher-skeleton uses {TASK_ID} placeholder but doesn't define it
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md defines {TASK_TYPE}, {AGENT_TYPE}, {DATA_FILE_PATH} in its placeholder list but uses {TASK_ID} in the commit message template without defining it. A fresh Queen filling in the skeleton would not know what value to substitute. Add {TASK_ID} to the placeholder definitions.

title: Review timestamp ownership contradicts between pantry.md and checkpoints.md
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 2 says the Pantry generates the review timestamp. checkpoints.md says the Queen generates it. A fresh Pantry and a fresh Pest Control would disagree on who owns this value. Pick one owner, document it, and update both files.

title: Review type names inconsistent between reviews.md and file naming
description: reviews.md section titles use 'Correctness Redux', 'Edge Cases (P2)' etc. but file names and checkpoints use 'correctness', 'edge-cases'. Drop 'Redux' and priority annotations from section titles to match the canonical short names used in file naming.

title: Timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS repeated 5+ times across files
description: The timestamp format is redefined in checkpoints.md at lines 31, 97, 152, 277, 443 and in pantry.md line 101. If the format ever changes, all 5+ locations must be updated. Define once at the top of checkpoints.md and reference it elsewhere.

title: Add glossary to RULES.md defining bead, epic, wave, dirt pusher
description: Terms like 'bead', 'epic', 'wave', 'dirt pusher', 'nitpicker' are used throughout orchestration files but never defined. A fresh agent must infer meaning from context. Add a Terminology section to RULES.md with concise definitions.

title: Epic artifact directory creation not enforced in RULES.md
description: RULES.md says the Queen creates epic directories at Step 2 but provides no explicit timing relative to Pantry spawn. reviews.md separately says to create review-reports/ before composing review prompts. A fresh Queen must manually create directories at different times with no consolidated checklist. Add a pre-spawn directory setup block to RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b.

title: Session directory passing mechanism not shown in RULES.md
description: RULES.md Step 0 generates SESSION_ID and creates the session dir. Scout.md expects {SESSION_DIR} as input. But RULES.md doesn't show HOW the Queen passes the session dir path to the Scout or any other agent. Add explicit instruction: pass session dir as a parameter in the spawn prompt.

title: Placeholder syntax inconsistent across orchestration files
description: Orchestration files mix three placeholder syntaxes: {curly-lowercase} for substitution, {UPPERCASE} for substitution, and <angle-brackets> for substitution. A fresh agent cannot tell which are literal vs to-be-substituted. Standardize: {UPPERCASE} = substitute, document convention at top of each file that uses placeholders.

title: Big Head data file structure not documented in pantry.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain but doesn't show the exact markdown format. The structure lives in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol. A fresh Pantry might produce the wrong format. Either inline the format or add an explicit cross-reference.

title: Pest Control CCO review section doesn't specify where commit range comes from
description: checkpoints.md CCO section for review prompts says 'Run git diff --name-only <first-commit>..<last-commit>' but doesn't explain how Pest Control receives these values. A fresh Pest Control agent would waste time looking for the source. Add: 'The Queen provides the commit range in the spawn prompt.'

title: Task ID variable naming inconsistent across templates
description: Some files use {task-id-suffix}, some use {task-id}, some use {TASK_ID}. These may refer to the same value or different values. A fresh agent cannot tell. Standardize variable names: pick one convention and find-replace across all templates.

title: MANDATORY keyword formatting inconsistent across templates
description: implementation.md uses plain 'MANDATORY', dirt-pusher-skeleton uses 'MANDATORY keyword present', checkpoints uses '(MANDATORY GATE)'. No consistent formatting. Pick one style (e.g. **MANDATORY**) and standardize.

title: Epic ID vs epic suffix terminology mixed across files
description: Some places say 'epic ID' (e.g. RULES.md), some say 'epic suffix' (e.g. checkpoints.md line 29: 'Use 3-char epic suffix'). These refer to the same value (e.g. 74g). Pick one term and standardize.

title: Pantry review return format doesn't show Big Head consolidation file
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 6 return table shows 4 review types but the Big Head consolidation data file path is listed separately below the table. Big-head-skeleton.md Step 0 says 'Read your consolidation brief from {DATA_FILE_PATH}'. A fresh Queen might miss the consolidation file path when parsing Pantry output. Make it prominent.

title: Big Head spawning mechanics unclear for fresh Queen
description: big-head-skeleton.md says Big Head is a team member (NOT a separate Task agent) but uses {PLACEHOLDER} syntax from Task tool templates. A fresh Queen would not know how to wire this through TeamCreate + SendMessage. Add explicit Queen-facing instructions showing the TeamCreate → SendMessage workflow with an example.

title: Task ID suffix terminology inconsistent across templates
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md uses <task-id>, pantry.md uses {task-id-suffix}, checkpoints.md uses both interchangeably. A fresh agent cannot tell if it should use the full ID (hs_website-74g.1) or just the suffix (74g.1). Standardize to one term across all orchestration files.

title: Missing prompts/ directory creation before Pantry writes
description: Pantry writes data files to {session-dir}/prompts/ but nobody creates that directory. RULES.md Step 0 creates {session-dir}/{task-metadata,previews} but prompts/ is not included. A fresh Pantry agent would fail on its first write attempt. Fix: add prompts/ to the mkdir -p command in RULES.md Step 0, or have Pantry create it.

title: Checkpoints CCO FAIL verdict format has no example
description: checkpoints.md shows PASS verdict format but no example of a FAIL verdict with specific check failures listed. A fresh Pest Control agent might format FAIL output incorrectly. Add a FAIL example showing check number, name, and evidence.

title: Wave concept used in RULES.md but never defined
description: RULES.md Hard Gates table references 'Next agent in wave' and checkpoints.md says 'BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave'. The concept of a wave (batch of parallel agents) is never defined. Add to glossary: 'Wave: a batch of agents spawned in parallel. Wave N completes before Wave N+1 begins.'

title: Big Head data file format split between pantry.md and reviews.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain (4 report paths, dedup protocol, bead filing instructions, consolidated output path) but the actual format/structure is in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. A fresh Pantry would need to cross-reference but there's no explicit pointer. Add cross-reference from pantry.md Step 4 to reviews.md.
